title: Factor schema + compliance gate + rubric + assembler into a reusable target-evidence-core library consumed by sibling card repos
context: The schema, compliance gate, grading rubric, and assembler built for this project are the shared engine that the sibling repos (ewing-drug-target-evidence, ewing-biomarker-evidence-cards) should consume rather than re-implement. Factoring these into a reusable target-evidence-core library is the highest-leverage spin-off and resolves the sibling overlap. The library must be agent-neutral (no vendor-specific code), publishable as an npm package, and consumed by the sibling repos. This is a future/backlog task that requires the core components to be stable first.
objective: Extract the card schema, compliance gate, grading rubric, and assembler engine from the project's packages into a standalone, publishable target-evidence-core npm package that sibling card repos can install and extend with their own adapters and panel configurations, without duplicating the compliance gate or rubric logic.
acceptanceCriteria: target-evidence-core package extracted and publishable as an npm package (or pnpm workspace package): includes the Evidence Card JSON Schema, the compliance gate (source allowlist + licence-class enforcement + per-assertion citation + identifiability guard), the grading rubric, and the core assembler with contradiction-surfacing and ID-mapping interfaces
Package is agent-neutral (no vendor-specific imports, no adapter code in the core); adapters in the consuming repos extend the core without modifying it
At least one sibling repo (ewing-drug-target-evidence or ewing-biomarker-evidence-cards) is migrated to consume target-evidence-core instead of maintaining its own copy of the schema/gate/rubric
Package is versioned with semver and its changelog documents breaking changes; consuming repos pin a specific version

The non-overlap rule is enforced: the package's exports include a mechanism for a consuming repo to declare which cancer indications it covers (so the cancer-wide panel can verify it is not double-covering the Ewing sibling's territory)
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass for the package and all consuming repos
output: A merged PR publishing the target-evidence-core npm package containing the shared schema, compliance gate, rubric, and assembler engine, with at least one sibling repo migrated to consume it — resolving the sibling overlap and making the engine reusable.
